g.title: XDRV
g.text: 18-50 VDC. Common-cathode signal: Mega D5/D7/D6 drive PUL+/DIR+/ENBL+; PUL-/DIR-/ENBL- jumpered to Mega GND. DIP SW1-8 = ON OFF ON OFF ON OFF OFF ON: 128 microstep (25600 pulse/rev), 1.92 A peak / 1.36 A RMS, SW4 OFF = half current at standstill. EN active-LOW (firmware).

g.title: YDRV
g.text: 18-50 VDC. Common-cathode signal: Mega D8/D10/D9 drive PUL+/DIR+/ENBL+; PUL-/DIR-/ENBL- jumpered to Mega GND. DIP SW1-8 = ON OFF OFF OFF ON OFF OFF ON: 128 microstep (25600 pulse/rev), 3.76 A peak / 2.69 A RMS, SW4 OFF = half current. Higher current because it drives TWO motors in parallel (~1.88 A each). EN active-LOW (firmware).

g.title: PSU
g.text: AC 110/220 V in; DC out 24 V / 15 A (360 W). Feeds both driver DC buses.

g.title: FUSE_X
g.text: On the +24 V leg to the X driver, close to the PSU. Upsized fast-blow so the driver's millisecond cap-inrush doesn't nuisance-trip; a hard short still pulls 15 A+ off the PSU and clears. Returns (V-) are unfused.

g.title: FUSE_Y
g.text: On the +24 V leg to the Y driver. Larger than X because the Y driver runs two motors in parallel (~2x current). Fast-blow upsized for inrush, as with X.

g.title: MOTOR_X
g.text: StepperOnline NEMA 17, 2.0 A/phase, 59 Ncm (84 oz-in), 48 mm, 4-lead (17HS19-2004S1). Coil A = A+ black / A- green; coil B = B+ red / B- blue. Drives the camera carriage. (+/- order only affects direction.)

g.title: MOTOR_Y1
g.text: StepperOnline NEMA 17 2.0 A/phase (17HS19-2004S1), GT2 belt. Coil A = A+ black / A- green; coil B = B+ red / B- blue.

g.title: MOTOR_Y2
g.text: Second vertical motor, wired in PARALLEL with Y1 on the YDRV outputs (both step together; that's why YDRV current is set ~2x). Same NEMA 17 / colors.

g.title: PIX_H
g.text: INPUT_PULLUP; OUT reads LOW when the slot is blocked

g.title: PIX_V
g.text: INPUT_PULLUP; OUT reads LOW when the slot is blocked

g.title: CAM
g.text: ~50 ms HIGH pulse fires capture (shorter is ignored)

g.title: RELAYS
g.text: Logic side: active-LOW; IN1 = shelf 1 … IN6 = shelf 6; coil supply (JD-VCC) external — see TODO. Contact side shown representatively: COM+ = fused +24 V into the contact common, LEDout = one switched output to a shelf LED string. Real board has per-channel COM/NO/NC; exact per-shelf LED contact wiring TBD.

g.title: FUSE_LED
g.text: Fuse — size to LED load, fast-blow (inline holder)

On the +24 V leg feeding the shelf-LED relay contacts. Amps = total LED watts / 24 V, next standard size up, kept below the relay's contact rating. LED load wattage still TBD (see hardware-open-questions.md).

g.title: LEDS
g.text: 24 V LED strings switched by the relay board off the main 24 V bus (operator confirmed). Shown as one representative string; per-shelf count / wattage / wiring TBD (see hardware-open-questions.md).
